Stucco Remediation in Austin, TX

When moisture gets behind your stucco, patching the surface only hides the problem. Star Stucco of Austin provides true stucco remediation — we find the source of water intrusion, remove all damaged material down to sound substrate, fix the underlying cause, and rebuild the system so it lasts. Free on-site moisture assessments throughout the Austin metro.

Get a Free Assessment Call (512) 706-9699

What Is Stucco Remediation?

Stucco remediation is the complete correction of a failing stucco system — not a cosmetic patch. When water penetrates behind stucco through cracks, failed sealant, or improper flashing, it becomes trapped against the substrate, leading to rot, mold, and structural damage that a surface repair will never solve. Remediation diagnoses where the water is getting in, removes everything that has been compromised, fixes the root cause, and rebuilds the wall correctly.

In Austin’s climate — expansive clay soils that move foundations, intense heat, and heavy spring storms — trapped moisture deteriorates walls quickly. If you suspect water damage, the worst thing to do is paint or patch over it. Learn more about typical pricing in our Austin stucco repair cost guide, or compare with our standard stucco repair services.

Signs Your Stucco Needs Remediation

Moisture damage gives warning signs before it becomes a structural problem. If you notice any of these, schedule an inspection right away.

Staining & Discoloration

Dark streaks or stains that reappear after painting indicate water is moving through or behind the stucco.

Soft or Spongy Spots

Areas that give when pressed signal that moisture has reached and weakened the substrate behind the stucco.

Bubbling & Delamination

Finish that bubbles, blisters, or separates from the wall is a classic sign of trapped moisture.

Efflorescence

White, chalky crystalline deposits on the surface mean water is migrating through the stucco and evaporating.

Our Stucco Remediation Process

A thorough, root-cause process that resolves moisture problems for good.

  1. 1

    Moisture Inspection & Testing

    We use moisture meters and probes to map the full extent of water intrusion — including hidden damage behind the surface — before any work begins.

  2. 2

    Source Identification

    We find exactly where water is entering: failed flashing, cracked sealant, missing kick-out diverters, window and door details, or roofline transitions.

  3. 3

    Removal of Damaged Material

    All compromised stucco, lath, building paper, and any rotted sheathing or framing are removed back to sound, dry material.

  4. 4

    Substrate & Structural Repair

    We replace damaged sheathing and address any framing rot, then allow the wall assembly to dry completely.

  5. 5

    Weather Barrier & Flashing Correction

    A new weather-resistant barrier and proper flashing details are installed to fix the original cause of the intrusion.

  6. 6

    Stucco System Rebuild

    The stucco system is reinstalled per spec — lath, scratch, brown, and finish coats — and the finish is color- and texture-matched to the surrounding wall.

  7. 7

    Sealing & Final Inspection

    All joints and penetrations are sealed, and we walk the completed remediation with you to confirm the problem is resolved.

Common Causes of Stucco Moisture Damage in Austin

Most stucco moisture problems trace back to a handful of failure points. Our remediation always corrects the cause, not just the symptom:

  • Failed or missing sealant at windows, doors, and penetrations
  • Improper or missing kick-out and step flashing at rooflines
  • Cracks from foundation movement on Austin’s expansive clay soils
  • Inadequate weep screed or drainage at the foundation line
  • Original installation defects in barrier-style stucco or EIFS
  • Roof, gutter, and downspout issues directing water onto walls

If your home or building has synthetic stucco, see our EIFS contractor services for moisture remediation specific to EIFS systems.

Real Moisture Testing

We never cover up wet stucco — every project starts with a documented moisture assessment.

Root-Cause Repairs

We fix the source of intrusion so the damage does not come back after the next storm.

Warranty-Backed Work

Licensed, insured, and warrantied remediation across the Austin metro.

Stop Stucco Water Damage Before It Spreads

Trapped moisture only gets worse — and more expensive — with time. Contact Star Stucco of Austin for a free moisture assessment and remediation estimate. As your local Austin stucco contractor, we’ll find the problem and fix it right.
